--- layout: default title: How to Use Advanced Filters nav_order: 330 --- {% raw %}

How to Use Advanced Filters

Step 1 – Start new filter

Choose an item to filter on – in this example, the Card Type.

We now have the filter NewProxCard as the card type filter.

Step 2 – Edit filter

Click on the Edit Filter button at the bottom right of the view.

Click on the And condition (root node of the filter) to call up the following menu.

Select the menu item Add Group.

You should now see two conditions: the main node condition And and a sub-node condition And (group).

Step 3 – Customize the filter as needed

Most conditions involving multiple columns and multiple values require groups of conditions. For example: Card = ‘My card’ AND (Area = ‘Floor 1’ OR Area = ‘Floor 2’) Note that in this filter the brackets include multiple values of the item Area. Multiple values of the same item must always be grouped by OR and different items are always grouped by AND with each group of item values. First, change the sub-group condition to Or, as shown, by clicking on the sub And item and selecting Or from the menu.

Next, add another comparison to this group by clicking on the “+” sign to the right of the Or group.

Next, change all the items and values as needed. If we want all cards except one type of card And located in two different areas, change the first item called [Card] and modify the Equals to Does not equal.

Next, change both the items labeled as [Access Permission] to Area.

Enter the value required (this is a manual operation, so care must be taken to enter the exact value needed).

Note that the filtered results are as required.

IMPORTANT: click Update to save the filter to the required view and Share if this view is to be used by all operators.
